current_activity: Plan the backend discovery lane as one read-only worker task that resolves MVP backend boundaries, API/persistence unknowns, and downstream design inputs without emitting final lane artifacts or outbound handoffs.

Plan a single read-only discovery task for the frontend lane to capture the MVP UI scope, frontend boundary assumptions, unresolved UI-facing questions, and downstream dependency notes for the React todo experience.
Fast-path discovery plan for React web frontend for creating, viewing, completing, editing, and deleting todo items: let the frontend manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Plan the middleware discovery lane as one read-only task that captures integration boundaries, open coordination questions, and external dependency assumptions for the todo application without producing implementation artifacts or outbound handoffs.
Fast-path discovery plan for Basic application integration and delivery workflow connecting frontend and backend: let the middleware manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Backend discovery scope is reconciled for the todo MVP: backend responsibility is limited to single-user todo CRUD persistence and serving, with a simple JSON REST contract and durable local storage as the preferred design direction. Remaining questions are narrowed to design-time choices around framework/runtime, persistence implementation, update semantics, and optional ordering.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Middleware discovery scope was reconciled from the injected planning inputs only. The lane is limited to frontend-backend integration concerns: defining the expected interaction surface between the React UI and a simple backend, coordinating request/response and persistence handoff expectations, and identifying the minimal delivery/review path. Frontend feature behavior and backend API/storage implementation remain external dependencies. Key later-phase unknowns remain the exact todo API shape, error/loading contract, persistence consistency expectations after mutations, validation depth, and the specific review/test checkpoints for integrated delivery.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Frontend discovery scope captured from the injected planning inputs only. MVP frontend behavior is limited to a simple React todo interface that can list existing items, create new items, edit item text, mark items complete, mark them incomplete, and delete items, with persistence expected through a backend API across reloads. Frontend ownership is limited to user-facing screens/components, local interaction states, and presentation of shared contract results; shared API and integration contract authority remains outside this lane. Frontend-specific unknowns to resolve in design are the exact loading/empty/error states, edit interaction pattern, minimal validation messaging, mutation feedback behavior, and accessibility/keyboard expectations. Later-phase dependencies are a stable CRUD/completion API contract, consistent error/result shapes, and confirmed integration behavior for persisted reload state.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

The backend design lane stays empty in this plan because the exact output contract authorizes no final lane outputs and no outbound handoffs for the design phase, so no worker task graph is scheduled.

Fast-path design plan for Basic application integration and delivery workflow connecting frontend and backend: let the middleware manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Reconciled the middleware design scope from injected discovery inputs into a single integration boundary: middleware owns the shared frontend-backend integration contract, runtime wiring, mediation of request/response and error flow expectations, and the minimal integrated validation path, while frontend and backend lanes retain authority over their own implementation contracts.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Defined the frontend MVP interaction contract for a simple React todo experience using only the injected planning and discovery inputs. The approved design fixes the UI boundaries and validation rules for list, create, edit, complete, uncomplete, and delete flows without expanding scope or introducing cross-lane dependencies.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Deliver the backend lane in two serialized worker tasks: first implement the persistent todo runtime, then finalize the backend-owned API contract, API tests, MVP report, and acceptance review bundle from the implemented runtime.
Plan a single backend lane to reconcile the approved design inputs into a working todo CRUD API with durable persistence, tests, and one acceptance handoff bundle.

Deliver the frontend MVP lane in three small worker tasks: bootstrap the React entry and API client, implement the todo interaction module, then add UI flow coverage and package the acceptance review bundle.

Lean MVP-build outline with one frontend delivery lane that implements the approved React todo experience, consumes the middleware-owned integration boundary, and hands a concrete review bundle to acceptance.
